experiment: adversarial length×register stress-test of shipped overconfidence_v0
ci_method: 2000-rep bootstrap on OLS coef; Wilson on error rates
verdict: DISCRIMINATION-ROBUST, THRESHOLD length-biased — the shipped scorer SEPARATES register within each length stratum (AUC short 0.83, long 0.86) but its score is shifted by length with register held fixed (is_long coef -1.93, 95% CI [-2.35, -1.495], NEGATIVE: longer scores LESS overconfident). MEASURED HARM at a fixed threshold: a careful SHORT answer is false-flagged 46% (CI [0.33, 0.596]) vs 4% when long; a cocky LONG answer is MISSED 46% (CI [0.33, 0.596]) vs 4% when short — length swings both errors ~42%. A length-aware guard (residualize S on log-words, 5-fold OUT-OF-SAMPLE) raises AUC 0.81->0.84 and cuts the FP length-disparity +0.42->-0.08 -> the fix is a deployment threshold guard, NOT a retrain. (NOTE: my prereg 'fooled' diagonal was the PROTECTED one given the negative coef -> 0/50; the corrected diagonal above is the real harm.)
